atoms.semantic_claim: Ethereum EVM configuration is wired to the Ritual-capable transaction environment and async-context traits so transaction references such as spc_calls, commitment_tx, and settlement_tx can reach EVM/precompile execution.

destinations.snippet: pub type EthEvmContext<DB> = AsyncContext<BlockEnv, TxEnv, CfgEnv, DB>
destinations.evidence: The destination Alloy EVM layer owns the async-capable Ethereum EVM context and TxEnv type selection.
Transaction-to-TxEnv conversion in the dependency propagates Ritual async metadata fields into the EVM transaction environment.
The underlying Revm context TxEnv carries the Ritual async/SPC metadata fields.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The source imported revm_context directly in reth-evm-ethereum; the v2.2 port pushes that wiring into the Alloy EVM and Revm dependency layers while EthEvmConfig stays generic over an EvmFactory whose Tx implements the required conversion traits.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Execute a recovered transaction carrying spc_calls, commitment_tx, and settlement_tx through EthEvmFactory and assert the resulting TxEnv preserves those fields for async precompile verification.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: confirmed covered. TxEnv carries commitment_tx, settlement_tx, and spc_calls in ritual-revm-internal, while ritual-alloy-evm-internal converts recovered Ritual transactions into that TxEnv.

atoms.semantic_claim: When replaying or executing an existing sealed block, the EVM execution context uses that block's header extra_data.

destinations.evidence: The destination context uses the existing block header extra_data directly; the source Option wrapper was removed because the destination execution context stores Bytes.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Source `Some(header.extra_data)` and destination `header.extra_data` both preserve the existing block header bytes at replay time; only the context type changed from optional to concrete bytes.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Replay a sealed block with non-default extra_data and assert EthBlockExecutionCtx carries exactly the block header bytes.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: confirmed covered. context_for_block clones the sealed block header extra_data into EthBlockExecutionCtx.

atoms.semantic_claim: When constructing the next block execution context, payload attributes extra_data is passed into the EVM execution context for block assembly.

destinations.evidence: The destination EVM config passes NextBlockEnvAttributes.extra_data into the execution context consumed by the block assembler.

atoms.equivalence_argument: At the EVM context boundary, the same dataflow exists. The remaining concern is upstream population of NextBlockEnvAttributes.extra_data from CL PayloadAttributes, tracked in file-0076.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Build a next-block context with dynamic extra_data in NextBlockEnvAttributes and assert the block assembler emits that value in the header.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: confirmed covered. context_for_next_block passes NextBlockEnvAttributes.extra_data into EthBlockExecutionCtx; file-0076 confirmed upstream CL population.

atoms.source_snippet: fn tx_env(&self, transaction: impl IntoTxEnv<TxEnvFor<Self>>) -> TxEnvFor<Self> { transaction.into_tx_env() }
atoms.semantic_claim: ConfigureEvm exposes transaction-to-TxEnv conversion through IntoTxEnv so callers can obtain the configured EVM transaction environment type.

destinations.snippet: fn tx_env(&self, transaction: impl IntoTxEnv<TxEnvFor<Self>>) -> TxEnvFor<Self> { transaction.into_tx_env() }
destinations.evidence: The same helper is now a default method on the shared ConfigureEvm trait, so EthEvmConfig no longer needs a local override.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The behavior moved from the concrete Ethereum config impl into the shared EVM trait default, preserving the same conversion call for all ConfigureEvm implementations.
atoms.notes: Trait-level coverage is preferable to a local duplicate method.

adversarial_reviews.reason: Mechanical review completed; remaining non-exact atoms are now confirmed covered, confirmed missing, intentionally absent, or support/test/telemetry only.

summary: Mechanical review complete. Async TxEnv metadata wiring and extra_data execution-context propagation are confirmed covered through the current Alloy/Revm dependency split and reth-evm-ethereum context builders.
notes: Queue entry 80 was verified as M\tcrates/ethereum/evm/src/lib.rs; only diff lines 16269-16327 were analyzed for this record. Mechanical review review-files-0077-0089-mechanical completed; all prior needs-review atoms have definitive verdicts.
